eventRef.showAs: Commission adoption of COM(2021)206 final by the College of Commissioners (Brussels)
Transmitted to the European Parliament and the Council of the European Union
European Economic and Social Committee opinion adopted (INT/940)
European Central Bank opinion adopted (CON/2021/40 — request received 3 Nov 2021, opinion adopted 29 Dec 2021)
European Committee of the Regions opinion adopted (CDR 2682/2021, SEDEC commission, rapporteur Guido Rink, NL)

EP joint committee referral: IMCO (Internal Market and Consumer Protection) + LIBE (Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs) — co-rapporteurs Brando Benifei (S&D, IMCO) and Dragoş Tudorache (Renew Europe, LIBE)
Council of the EU general approach adopted (Czech Presidency)
IMCO/LIBE joint committee report adopted (84 in favour, 7 against, 12 abstentions) — substantially amends Commission proposal
EP plenary vote on negotiating mandate (Strasbourg) — 499 in favour, 28 against, 93 abstentions; entry into trilogue negotiations approved
Inter-institutional trilogue negotiations started (Commission + EP + Council)

Trilogue political agreement reached after marathon talks across 6, 7 and 8 December 2023 (Spanish Presidency of the Council; Carme Artigas, Spanish Secretary of State for Digitalisation and AI, led negotiations for the Council)
COREPER (Permanent Representatives Committee) unanimous endorsement of trilogue compromise
IMCO/LIBE joint committee final vote on trilogue text (71 in favour, 8 against, 7 abstentions)
EP first reading position adopted in plenary (Strasbourg) — 523 in favour, 46 against, 49 abstentions; published as P9_TA(2024)0138
Council of the EU formal final adoption (unanimously) — text matches the EP first reading position so no second reading needed
Signed by Roberta Metsola (EP President) and the President of the Council; act becomes Reg (EU) 2024/1689

TLCOrganization.showAs: EP Committee on the Internal Market and Consumer Protection (IMCO)
EP Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs (LIBE)

TLCPerson.showAs: Dragoş Tudorache (Renew Europe, Romania) — LIBE co-rapporteur

TLCRole.showAs: EP rapporteur (lead committee member responsible for the file)

proprietary.akndiff:billStatus: Procedure completed — Act adopted as Reg (EU) 2024/1689 of 13 June 2024
proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
Friction log for EU bill com-2021-206 — Phase 1. Each note maps to a SPECIFIC schema field that strains under EU reality.
1. SPONSOR IS THE EUROPEAN COMMISSION (organization). Same friction as Spanish Proyecto de Ley 121/000036 where sponsor is Gobierno. BillTable.sponsors expects person shape {name, role, party, chamber, externalId}; we use the institution. CROSS-COUNTRY CONFIRMED: schema needs polymorphic sponsor (person | organization).
2. RAPPORTEUR ROLE NOT IN SCHEMA. EP bills have rapporteurs (lead committee member responsible for the file) — distinct from sponsor. AI Act had TWO co-rapporteurs because of the joint IMCO/LIBE committee setup. We carry them as akndiff:rapporteur extension elements with committee= attribute. BillTable.sponsors cannot represent the sponsor/rapporteur distinction. Same structural gap noted in original ES checklist for EU.
3. JOINT COMMITTEE REFERRAL. EP referred the file JOINTLY to IMCO and LIBE under Rule 58 (formerly Rule 55). Schema's chamber field on events accepts a string ("parliament") but doesn't model multiple committees within one chamber being co-responsible.
4. CONSULTATIVE OPINIONS BY EESC, ECB, CoR are formal procedural steps required by the Treaties (TFEU Arts 304, 282(5), 307). They produce documents (opinions) that are NOT bills, amendments, debates or any other modeled type. We capture them as eventRef with chamber= the committee name; no document side is modeled. Schema has no opinion type — same gap noted in AI Act researchNote 12 of the prior version (now consolidated here).
5. PARALLEL TRACK BICAMERALISM (vs ES sequential). ES events flow Congreso → Senado → Congreso (linear). EU bill events show PARALLEL tracks: EP committee work (2022-2023) overlapped with Council work (Council general approach 2022-12-06 happened BEFORE EP first reading mandate 2023-06-14). They reconciled in trilogue. The chamber attribute on eventRef captures which institution acted but does not model that EP and Council were working SIMULTANEOUSLY on the SAME draft. Cross-country friction confirmed (also flagged in AI Act researchNote 9).
6. TRILOGUE = NEW INSTITUTIONAL FORM. The trilogue is an informal inter-institutional negotiation between Commission, EP, Council that produces the political agreement. Not a bill, amendment, or debate; events happen in closed session. We use chamber= "trilogue" as a non-canonical value. Schema has no concept of inter-institutional negotiation as a phase.
7. PROCEDURE FILE 2021/0106(COD) IS THE CROSS-INSTITUTIONAL REFERENCE. Used by Commission, EP, Council, OEIL, EUR-Lex uniformly. ES expediente (121/000036) is per-chamber. We carry in akndiff:procedureFile but it's not a real column. Cross-country friction (also in AI Act researchNote 10).
8. EP FIRST READING ADOPTED ON FIRST TRY (no Council second reading). The EU's ordinary legislative procedure normally has up to three readings. Here the trilogue agreement let EP and Council adopt the SAME text in first reading — final adoption in single round each. Loader-extracted events stop at #passed_second_chamber even though there's no real "second chamber reading" — Council just formally adopts. Status normalization is fine but the event type vocabulary (passed_second_chamber) is misleading for EU.
9. P9_TA(2024)0138 IS THE EP "POSITION" — equivalent to ES dictamen but produced by the plenary, not a committee. Includes the consolidated text + the recorded vote (523-46-49). Our amendment XML (ep-p9-ta-2024-0138.xml) models this; here we link to it via the eventRef showAs.
10. NO "WITHDRAWN BY COMMISSION" PATH MODELED. The Commission can withdraw its proposal at any time before adoption (didn't happen here). The schema's BillStatus union has 'withdrawn' but no special semantics for "Commission-withdrawn" vs "EP-rejected". Out of scope for AI Act but a Phase 2 friction.

longTitle.p: Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (Artificial Intelligence Act) and amending certain Union legislative acts. {SEC(2021) 167 final} - {SWD(2021) 84 final} - {SWD(2021) 85 final}. COM(2021) 206 final, 2021/0106(COD), Brussels, 21.4.2021.

preamble.p: EXPLANATORY MEMORANDUM. The Commission's proposal is accompanied by an Explanatory Memorandum (EM) and an Impact Assessment (SWD(2021)84 + 85). The proposal aims to lay down harmonised rules for the development, placement on the market and use of AI systems in the Union, following a proportionate risk-based approach. Subsequent to this proposal, the EP (in its 2023-06-14 mandate and 2024-03-13 first reading) and the Council (in its 2022-12-06 general approach) substantially amended the text. The final adopted version is in Reg (EU) 2024/1689.

article.heading: Articulated text proposed by the Commission

content.p: The Commission's proposed articulated text was published as COM(2021)206 final on 21 April 2021 (CELEX:52021PC0206 — full text accessible at EUR-Lex). It contained 85 articles + 9 annexes structured into 12 titles, addressing: prohibited AI practices, high-risk AI systems classification (Annex III, then 8 areas), high-risk requirements (Title III Chapter 2), provider/user obligations, conformity assessment, EU database, transparency obligations for limited-risk systems, codes of conduct, governance (European Artificial Intelligence Board), penalties, and final provisions.

The proposal was substantially amended throughout the trilogue (2023-06-21 to 2023-12-08) following the EP's first reading negotiating mandate (P9_A(0138)/2023, 14 June 2023) and the Council's general approach (15698/22, 6 December 2022). Major additions during trilogue: full Chapter V on general-purpose AI models with systemic risk (initially absent), expansion of prohibited practices in Article 5, fundamental rights impact assessment by deployers (Article 27), AI Office and Scientific Panel under Title VI, three-tier penalty system, and the staged application schedule across 36 months.

The final consolidated text adopted on 2024-03-13 (EP) and 2024-05-21 (Council) — published as P9_TA(2024)0138 (EP first reading position, see amendment doc) and finally as Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — has 113 articles + 13 annexes, organised into 13 chapters. Substantial structural growth vs the original 85-article proposal.
